text: Add Member Payment Allocation Waterfall: untargeted member payments apply to loan mora/fees, loan interest, loan principal, overdue aportes, current aporte, then require a one-tap treasurer decision for extra money.
reason: Pilot feedback showed that recording a regular aporte can leave older obligations late; the rule must live in the substrate/rule registry so aporte, prestamo, atrasos, history, statements, liquidity, and reconciliation stay consistent.

notes: Created implementation-ready BR-26 story and plan from pilot feedback; artifact coverage includes contributions, loan repayments, A/R aging, liquidity, reconciliation, statements, history, idempotency, and the US-100 rule substrate.
BR-26 waterfall applies loan fees, loan interest, loan principal, overdue aportes, current aporte, then explicit extra-money decision.
Default aporte flow routes through grouped payment service and shows confirmation/extra decision when needed.
Targeted overdue payment can redirect to disclosed higher-priority obligations while preserving BR-26 ordering.
Loan-targeted payments disclose conflicts and payment allocations across loan fee, interest, and principal.
Grouped payment receipts narrate readable split details with contribution cycle labels.
Monthly member statements group BR-26 receipts once with split details and avoid duplicate child contribution rows.
payment_receipt enforces org/client_request idempotency; payment_allocation enforces receipt sort-order uniqueness in SQL and Drizzle metadata.
BR-26 implemented, reviewed, and verified with targeted domain/db/web tests plus full type-check, lint, and build.

title: Tighten substrate sprint-plan and generator source-of-truth rules
description: After running infra/scripts/sync-from-nous.sh on 2026-07-10, SPRINT_PLAN.md regenerated but still renders completed or externally deferred work as ambiguous 🔨/⬜ markers without a legend, and US-126 local done/ac_verify evidence from .nous-feedback.jsonl is not reflected in the sprint queue. The same sync also regenerated apps/web/src/components/shell/nav-items.gen.ts from an unrelated sales/CRM nav shape even though docs/specs/07c_navigation_map.json contains Mi Banquito app_shell data. Please tighten substrate instructions and gates: define status icon semantics, distinguish done_with_external_deferral from active work, require agents to reconcile .nous-feedback.jsonl before reopening stories, make sync warn/fail when done events are not consumed by SPRINT_PLAN.md, and validate generated app artifacts against project-specific invariants before writing them.

title: Sidebar generator emitted unrelated CRM/Sales navigation into Mi Banquito
description: During the 2026-07-10 sync-from-nous regeneration, the substrate wrote apps/web/src/components/shell/nav-items.gen.ts with unrelated CRM/Sales items such as Dashboard, Pipeline, Accounts, Team, Forecast, Strategy, SELLER/MANAGER/DIRECTOR/ADMIN roles, and /dashboard or /pipeline routes. This would have broken Mi Banquito's sidebar and hidden the real treasurer/admin routes. The local change was intentionally excluded before commit. Please add a hard project-invariant gate before writing generated app artifacts: Mi Banquito nav output must include expected routes like /socias, /aportes, /prestamos, /atrasos, /cierre, /estados, /liquidez, /reparto, /admin and role values TESORERA/PLATFORM_OPERATOR; it must reject foreign role/route vocabularies and fail sync loudly instead of writing contaminated files.

method: service negative tests plus database transfer trigger catalog verification

method: paginated large-fixture and deterministic backpressure tests

The single Vercel app cannot embed the external 10k-line Nous substrate and its private Nous DB. Production uses a documented authenticated NOUS_DRIFT_RUNNER_URL contract; provisioning and live secret evidence remain deployment prerequisites.

method: signed-cookie session, RLS, ALS isolation, and structural write-gate tests

method: proxy and real domain write rejection tests
notes: Unsafe requests, cron GETs, login/callback, and writable tenant transactions return Impersonation is read-only before any row or audit write.
Persistent banner renders across tenant pages; end inserts append-only termination, clears cookie, fixed-redirects, and duplicate end is idempotent.
Start and end audits preserve the original reason and commit atomically with append-only lifecycle events.

The source Impersonation table is append-only, so end state is recorded in append-only impersonation_termination instead of updating ended_at; legacy start rows keep nullable target_membership_id/expires_at while all new rows populate both.

Substrate source-of-truth conflict found in production: US-029 AC-4/AC-5 and its technical notes define /aportes/registrar as a contribution-only write where no loan rule fires, while US-126 repurposes the same route as the default untargeted BR-26 member-payment waterfall. SCR-record-contribution.json was not regenerated for US-126: its title, action, submit label, description, and meta.stories still describe only an aporte and omit the allocation workflow.

title: BR-26 screen contract lacks allocation and success states
description: US-126 AC-5 requires confirmation and success states to show the exact payment split in plain Spanish, but the authoritative SCR-record-contribution screen has no preview, allocation, receipt, or success-state sections. The generated implementation therefore discarded the persisted receipt/allocation result and left the treasurer on a screen labeled Registrar aporte, making a valid loan-first allocation look like a failed contribution. Tighten the substrate by explicitly superseding conflicting US-029 criteria, updating the TOON screen and navigation copy to Registrar pago de socia, and requiring receipt_id plus allocation-line rendering in the success contract.

title: Member-payment errors have no observable contract
description: The member-payment Server Action caught every validation, domain, constraint, and read-model-refresh failure and redirected to one generic Spanish sentence without logging a stable error code. The production POST returned 303, but logs could not distinguish expected extra-money confirmation, slip validation, account/config/cycle failures, idempotency conflicts, or transaction rollback. Add a generated error-code taxonomy, safe structured logging requirements, and exact user-facing recovery copy to the story/screen contract.

title: Generated action test violates the real-dependency testing standard
description: The /aportes/registrar action test mocks createPaymentService, despite docs/dev-guide/TESTING.md prohibiting mocks of code we own. It asserts only that the mock was called, so it cannot detect failures across allocation, PostgreSQL constraints, receipt/allocation children, read-model refresh, audit, or success rendering. Require a real PostgreSQL command-to-projection integration test and a thin authenticated browser journey for every critical money-entry form before marking the story done.

method: Adversarial production-log trace plus action/page regression tests
notes: A successful member payment now redirects to a receipt-filtered history view with a visible success status and persisted BR-26 allocation details; known failures retain stable recovery codes and unmapped failures emit structured diagnostics.
Payment-entry repair passed type-check, warning-free lint, full workspace tests, focused action/page/history regressions, and the Next.js production build.

title: BR-26 dropped the automatic current-cycle invariant
description: Production exposed a cross-story contract gap: US-029's contribution service automatically creates the dated monthly contribution cycle when none exists, but US-126's replacement BR-26 payment service assumed the cycle was already seeded. With no open cycle, the allocator classified the entire aporte as extra money; selecting Aporte extra / ahorro then failed because persistence requires a cycle_id. Tighten the substrate by carrying the automatic current-cycle invariant into US-126 ACs and requiring a real-PostgreSQL test from payment command through receipt, contribution, read-model refresh, and selected account balance when the cycle is initially absent.

notes: With no pre-seeded current cycle, preview remains read-only, record atomically creates the dated cycle plus receipt/allocation/contribution, refreshes derived models, exposes the exact selected fund-account balance, and returns no cross-tenant balances.
Current-cycle payment repair passed workspace type-check, lint, full real-database test suite, focused BR-26 regressions, and Next.js production build. No schema migration required.

title: Closed monthly snapshot exposed mutation controls
description: The monthly-close domain correctly locks a closed period and preserves its balance snapshot, but the generated SCR-monthly-close UI continued rendering Guardar conciliacion, pending-regularization, annotation, and Cerrar mes controls. This made an immutable historical June close appear editable and suggested that later July transactions should change its figures. Tighten the screen contract with an explicit closed state that renders only the period summary and archived statement/share controls, with no write forms or pending-work panels.

method: Closed-state render regression plus cutoff review
notes: A closed period renders its immutable summary and statement archive without reconciliation, annotation, pending-regularization, or close controls. Balance derivation remains bounded by cycle.closesOn, so July transactions do not alter the June snapshot.

Closed monthly-view repair passed focused render regressions, workspace type-check, lint, full test suite with real PostgreSQL configuration, and the Next.js production build. No schema migration required.

title: Pending dashboard card routed to an archived close instead of regularization
description: When the latest closable period was already closed, the treasurer home correctly counted current-period pending deposits but linked the card to SCR-monthly-close. The closed-state contract correctly hides mutation controls, leaving no discoverable path to reconcile the current pending item. Tighten the cross-screen contract so any global pending-regularization entry navigates to SCR-record-movement; period-specific rows may link there with regularizesKind and regularizesId preselection.

method: Dashboard navigation regression plus production read-model inspection
notes: The global pending card opens SCR-record-movement, where actionable pending deposits are listed and the regularization form targets active group-fund accounts. Production currently contains one actionable USD 10 Toitq contribution in the non-group Guayaquil account.

Pending-regularization navigation repair passed focused home and movement-screen regressions, type-check, lint, full workspace tests against a freshly migrated PostgreSQL database, and the Next.js production build. No schema migration required.

title: Authenticated proxy corrupted successful private export streams
description: Production proved that export generation, private Blob upload, SHA-256 verification, and data.exported audit all succeeded, while the immediate durable download returned 500 after the route emitted 200. The Auth0 proxy matcher covered binary export downloads even though the route already enforces requirePlatformOperator, causing the proxy layer to turn a healthy streamed response into a false generation_failed result. Tighten the substrate with an explicit streaming-route/proxy contract: route-authorized private downloads bypass response middleware, signed generation remains proxied, no in-memory buffering is allowed, and the matcher behavior is verified with Next's real matcher evaluator.

method: Production request trace, exact private Blob read, and Next matcher regression
notes: The failed export committed one 26,623-byte ZIP whose production Blob bytes match the stored SHA-256. The unsigned route-authorized download now bypasses the proxy; signed generation and the export page remain proxied and platform-operator authorization remains enforced in the route.

Export download proxy repair passed type-check, lint, all clean-CI workspace tests and contracts, Next.js production build, and Playwright smoke tests. The exact production private Blob was independently read and its size and SHA-256 matched the committed audit. No schema migration required.

Production follow-up: Next unstable_doesMiddlewareMatch reported the complex export-download negative-lookahead as excluded, but Vercel's deployed serverless middleware still wrapped the durable ZIP response and converted route 200 into middleware 500. Streaming-route acceptance must include deployed-runtime evidence, and matcher-only tests are insufficient for this contract.

Adversarial export repair verifies the durable GET bypasses Auth0 middleware at runtime while signed generation and the export page remain proxied; empty signed-request values follow the route's durable-download semantics; route-level platform-operator authorization remains mandatory.

Production replay evidence: a signed GET generated export 8bc1ce4a-e49f-4325-b9cb-533b81a6b8ea, committed data.exported and returned 303; the durable archive returned 200; then a second GET returned generation_failed because the non-overwriting Blob path already existed. Export generation uses GET under a PWA whose default runtime policy is NetworkFirst. Tighten the substrate: side-effecting GETs must be explicitly NetworkOnly and idempotent, generated service-worker artifacts must be inspected, retry/replay ACs must assert one durable result, and generation catches must emit structured phase/error logs without signed tokens.

Replay repair validates the signed request before tenant-scoped history lookup, redirects an existing successful export before preparation, leaves failed/no-audit requests retryable, forces all tenant export GETs through the first NetworkOnly service-worker rule, and records structured generation/download exceptions in Vercel and Sentry without request tokens.
